subtitle: How a Marriage in Magadha Lit the Torch of the Gupta Age

panels.shot: low-angle heroic medium close-up with wide backdrop of the Ganga and Pataliputra’s timber palisades
panels.action: Chandragupta I the First stands in royal ornaments, one hand resting on a large golden coin bearing faint figures of a king and queen, the hazy outline of Pataliputra and the broad Ganga stretching behind him under a pale dawn sky.

dialogue.text: In the misty plains of Magadha, Chandragupta the First bound his house to the Licchavis, and from a single marriage and a single coin, an age of Guptas began.

i18n.hi: धुंधले मगध के मैदानों में चन्द्रगुप्त प्रथम ने अपना घर लिच्छवियों से जोड़ा, और एक शादी व एक सिक्के से गुप्तों का युग जन्मा।

panels.shot: high, sweeping bird’s-eye over the eastern Gangetic plain
panels.action: The camera soars above the braided channels of the Ganga and Son rivers, fields of early monsoon green stretching to the horizon, small towns and forest patches scattered across the flat land under a humid, clouded sky.

dialogue.text: Four centuries after the Mauryas, the eastern Ganga plain lay divided—Magadha here, forest there, small kings and clans watching one another across rivers and roads.

i18n.hi: मौर्यों के चार सौ बरस बाद, गंगा का पूर्वी मैदान बँटा पड़ा था—इधर मगध, उधर जंगल, बीच में छोटे राजा–कुल नदी–सड़कों पर एक-दूसरे को तौलते।

panels.shot: mid-wide shot inside a modest yet dignified royal court
panels.action: Within a timber-pillared hall in Pataliputra, young Chandragupta I the First stands beside a simple throne, wearing restrained jewels, while a court priest reads a copper deed; courtiers in plain cotton observe in wary silence.

dialogue.text: The Guptas were still one house among many. Sri Gupta and Ghatotkaca had ruled small tracts as maharajas; their heir Chandragupta would inherit not an empire, but a corner of Magadha.

i18n.hi: तब गुप्त भी कई घरानों में बस एक थे। श्रीगुप्त और घटोत्कच छोटे भूभाग के महाराजा थे; उनका वारिस चन्द्रगुप्त किसी सम्राज्य नहीं, बस मगध के एक कोने का मालिक था।

panels.shot: close-up on Chandragupta’s face, lit by lamplight
panels.action: A close view of Chandragupta I seated alone at night by a low oil lamp, his thoughtful eyes reflecting the flame, a rolled palm-leaf lying unopened beside him on a simple wooden desk.

dialogue.text: Yet he saw what others did not. Power in Magadha would not return through conquest alone. It must be woven, as cloth is woven, through blood, marriage and the weight of old names.

i18n.hi: पर उसने वह देखा जो औरों ने नहीं। मगध की ताकत सिर्फ जीत से नहीं लौटेगी। इसे बुनना होगा, जैसे कपड़ा, खून, ब्याह और पुराने नामों के भार से।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of a Licchavi stronghold-town
panels.action: A fortified hilltop settlement rises above the plain, its wooden walls and watchtowers bearing fluttering standards, with carved gateways hinting at older republican days, while below, caravans and villagers move along a dusty road.

dialogue.text: To the north and east lay the Licchavis, heirs to ancient republics, now holding their own territories and prestige. Their name still carried the echo of vaunted gana-sabhas and old alliances.

i18n.hi: उत्तर–पूर्व में थे लिच्छवि, प्राचीन गणों के वारिस, अब अपनी धरती और मान के मालिक। उनके नाम में अब भी पुरानी गणसभाओं और पुराने मेलों की गूँज थी।

panels.shot: medium shot from behind Chandragupta as he consults elders
panels.action: Seen from behind, Chandragupta I sits on a low platform while older counselors, draped in scholarly robes, gesture over a rough map marked with rivers and small pebbles for towns.

dialogue.text: Modern scholars see his realm as modest—Magadha, Prayaga, parts of Bihar. But even a modest king could reach for greatness by choosing his bonds wisely.

i18n.hi: आज के विद्वान उसकी हद बस इतनी मानते हैं—मगध, प्रयाग, बिहार के हिस्से। पर छोटा राजा भी अगर रिश्ते सही चुने, तो ऊँचाई छू सकता है।

panels.shot: slow push-in on Chandragupta receiving a scroll
panels.action: A Licchavi envoy in fine cotton hands a rolled message, bound with a simple seal, to Chandragupta I, who accepts it with measured composure while attendants watch intently from the edges of the hall.

dialogue.text: Somewhere between the courts of Magadha and the Licchavis, a proposal ripened—an alliance not sealed by bloodshed, but by a bride.

i18n.hi: मगध और लिच्छवि दरबारों के बीच कहीं एक प्रस्ताव पक रहा था—संधि जो खून से नहीं, दुल्हन से तय होनी थी।

panels.shot: medium shot imagining the future, with subtle double exposure of banners
panels.action: Chandragupta I stands at an open balcony of the palace, looking out toward the river, while in the hazy distance faint ghostly images of Gupta and Licchavi standards appear side by side above a shared bastion.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_i
dialogue.text: Gupta strength with Licchavi name…the plain will listen.

i18n.hi: गुप्त की ताकत, लिच्छवि का नाम…पूरा मैदान कान लगाएगा।

panels.shot: wide ceremonial shot of the wedding pavilion
panels.action: Under a richly decorated pavilion draped with textiles and garlands, Chandragupta I stands beside an unseen veiled bride, only her hand and ornaments visible, as sacred fire burns low and priests chant before them.

dialogue.text: Around 319 of the Common Era, in a ceremony remembered only in echoes, Chandragupta took Kumaradevi of the Licchavis as his queen, fusing two houses under sacred fire.

i18n.hi: लगभग ३१९ ईस्वी में, एक अब धुंधली पड़ चुकी रस्म में, चन्द्रगुप्त ने लिच्छवि कुमारदेवी को रानी बनाया, दो घरानों को अग्नि के फेरे में जोड़ा।

panels.shot: intimate medium shot of king and queen seated together, seen from the side
panels.action: Inside a quiet inner chamber, Chandragupta I sits alongside his queen, shown in partial profile with Licchavi-style ornaments, both composed yet slightly formal, their hands resting near each other on a shared armrest.

dialogue.text: Kumaradevi brought more than grace. She brought lineage, claim, and the silent consent of Licchavi chiefs, who now watched Gupta fortunes as their own.

i18n.hi: कुमारदेवी केवल रूप नहीं लाई। वह वंश, दावा, और चुपचाप सहमति लाई—लिच्छवि सरदारों की, जो अब गुप्त किस्मत को अपना मानकर देख रहे थे।

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle shot in the court as a new title is proclaimed
panels.action: In the great hall, Chandragupta I stands as a priest unfurls a cloth inscribed with titles; courtiers bow slightly deeper than before, and a drum rests silent at the edge, capturing a poised moment of recognition.

dialogue.text: With his position secured, he took a higher style. No longer merely maharaja, like Sri Gupta and Ghatotkaca, he declared himself maharajadhiraja—great king over other kings.

i18n.hi: स्थिति पक्की होते ही उसने ऊँचा ख़िताब लिया। श्रीगुप्त और घटोत्कच की तरह केवल महाराजा नहीं, खुद को महाराजाधिराज—राजाओं का राजा घोषित किया।

panels.shot: close-up of a court scholar’s hand annotating a palm-leaf
panels.action: A learned scribe, his hand steady, inscribes Chandragupta’s new title on a fresh palm-leaf record, while in the blurred background the king’s figure is visible in conversation with advisers.

dialogue.text: His reach was still bounded by Magadha, Prayaga, nearby lands. Yet titles mattered. They told neighbors that this small house now thought and spoke like a dynasty.

i18n.hi: उसकी पकड़ अब भी मगध, प्रयाग और आस–पास तक थी। पर पदवी मायने रखती थी। वह पड़ोसियों को बताती कि यह छोटा घर अब खुद को वंश समझकर सोचता–बोलता है।

panels.shot: low, intense shot inside a mint workshop
panels.action: In a dim mint room, Chandragupta I leans over a workbench as artisans hold a carved die; a disk of gleaming gold lies ready to be struck, tools and crucibles scattered around.

dialogue.text: Then came his boldest stroke—not on battlefield, but in metal. He ordered a coin that would speak for both throne and marriage.

i18n.hi: फिर आया उसका सबसे साहसी दाँव—ना मैदान में, बल्कि धातु पर। उसने ऐसा सिक्का गढ़वाया जो सिंहासन और विवाह दोनों की भाषा बोले।

panels.shot: extreme close-up of a hammer poised over a gold blank
panels.action: A gold blank rests on the lower die, the upper die showing faint reversed figures of a king and queen; a craftsman’s muscular hands hold the hammer high, Chandragupta’s face watching from the edge of the frame.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_i
dialogue.text: Strike it true. Let the world see who we are.

i18n.hi: ठोंको इसे सटीक। दुनिया देख ले, हम कौन हैं।

panels.shot: macro shot of the freshly struck coin cooling on a cloth
panels.action: A bright gold coin lies cooling on a folded cloth; its tiny relief shows a crowned king and queen side by side, with a short legend band hinting at letters that would read ‘Lichchhavayah’.

dialogue.text: On that gold, king and queen stood together, named together. Around them, the legend invoked the Licchavis—lichchhavayah—making Kumaradevi’s clan a seal upon Gupta rule.

i18n.hi: उस सोने पर राजा–रानी साथ खड़े, साथ नामित थे। चारों ओर लेख में ‘लिच्छवयः’ कहकर लिच्छवियों को पुकारा गया, कुमारदेवी के वंश को गुप्त राज की मुहर बनाया गया।

panels.shot: medium shot of merchants examining the coin in a bustling marketplace
panels.action: In a crowded bazaar, merchants and money-changers pass the new coin from hand to hand, squinting at the paired figures, while a Gupta official observes quietly from a shaded corner.

dialogue.text: Across markets and shrines, those coins moved. They paid soldiers, priests, and merchants, each transaction whispering the same story—Gupta king, Licchavi queen, a joint claim to the heartland.

i18n.hi: बाज़ारों और देवालयों में वही सिक्के घूमे। सैनिक, पुजारी, व्यापारी सबको उन्हीं से दाम मिले—हर लेन–देन में वही फुसफुसाहट: गुप्त राजा, लिच्छवि रानी, मिल–जुला दिल की धरती पर दावा।

panels.shot: medium shot of Chandragupta and Kumaradevi presented with young Samudragupta
panels.action: In a sunlit courtyard, Chandragupta I and his queen sit on a low platform while a young boy, Samudragupta, stands before them holding a script tablet, his posture alert and curious.

dialogue.text: From this union came a son, Samudragupta, who would carve his name across the subcontinent. His own poet, Harishena, would later praise Chandragupta and remember the Licchavi tie.

i18n.hi: इसी मिलन से जन्मा बेटा समुद्रगुप्त, जिसने अपना नाम पूरे उपमहाद्वीप पर उकेरा। उसके कवि हरीशेन ने बाद में चन्द्रगुप्त की स्तुति की और लिच्छवि नाते को याद रखा।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of the Allahabad Pillar imagined in its later Gupta setting
panels.action: The towering stone of the future Allahabad Pillar rises against an open sky, its inscriptions partially visible, with a faint superimposed figure of Samudragupta overseeing scribes at its base.

dialogue.text: On the pillar at Prayaga, Harishena’s words carved a lineage—Sri Gupta, Ghatotkaca, Chandragupta, Kumaradevi. The record fixed what the coin had first proclaimed.

i18n.hi: प्रयाग स्तंभ पर हरीशेन के शब्दों ने वंश लिख दिया—श्रीगुप्त, घटोत्कच, चन्द्रगुप्त, कुमारदेवी। अभिलेख ने वही पक्का किया जो सिक्के ने पहले कह दिया था।

panels.shot: wide contemplative shot of Chandragupta on the ramparts at dusk
panels.action: At dusk on the city wall, Chandragupta I stands alone looking over the darkening fields and river, the faint lights of villages dotting the landscape, his figure steady yet unassuming against the deepening sky.

dialogue.text: He did not rule all India. His lands were close, his city small. Yet with a marriage, a title, and a coin, Chandragupta the First laid the quiet foundation of the Gupta age.

i18n.hi: वह पूरे भारत का शासक नहीं था। उसकी ज़मीन पास–पास, नगरी छोटी थी। पर एक विवाह, एक उपाधि और एक सिक्के से चन्द्रगुप्त प्रथम ने गुप्त युग की शांत नींव रख दी।

title_i18n.en: Chandragupta I and the Licchavi Bride

subtitle_i18n.en: How a Marriage in Magadha Lit the Torch of the Gupta Age
subtitle_i18n.hi: मगध की शादी जिसने गुप्त युग की मशाल जलाई
